label: Survey Research
definition: Primary data collected via structured online survey instrument distributed to target population

label: Benchmarking
definition: Comparative analysis using Aberdeen Competitive Framework to classify organizations as Laggard (30%), Industry Norm (50%), or Best in Class (20%)

label: Qualitative Analysis
definition: Expert judgment, interview-based findings, and qualitative interpretation of survey data and industry trends

label: Case Study
definition: Specific organizational example cited to illustrate a finding or trend (e.g., J.P. Morgan Chase/IBM)

label: Retrospective Assessment
definition: Post-publication assessment of prediction accuracy or outcome verification conducted during archival ingest

label: Strategy Classification
definition: Categorization of organizational approach within the Aberdeen Competitive Framework tiers (Laggard, Industry Norm, Best in Class)

label: Viability Prediction
definition: Forward-looking assessment of technology, practice, or market trend viability

label: Actual Outcome
definition: Verified post-study outcome that can be compared against original predictions or assessments

label: Technology Assessment
definition: Evaluation of a technology's maturity, applicability, or strategic role at the time of study

label: Benchmark Result
definition: Quantitative finding from the benchmarking analysis comparing organizational performance tiers

label: Framework Factor
definition: Element within the PACE (Pressures, Actions, Capabilities, Enablers) analytical framework

label: Market Data
definition: Quantitative survey result describing market characteristics, respondent demographics, or industry statistics

label: Expert Opinion
definition: Interpretive judgment by the study author or cited authority based on evidence and expertise

label: High Confidence
definition: Finding directly supported by primary survey data or clearly stated in report with quantitative evidence

label: Medium Confidence
definition: Finding supported by qualitative analysis, expert interpretation, or retrospective assessment with reasonable evidence

label: Low Confidence
definition: Finding based on limited evidence, extrapolation, or indirect inference

label: Benchmark Report
definition: Comparative study using standardized framework to evaluate organizational practices and performance across industry tiers

label: Survey Research
definition: Primary research methodology using structured questionnaires distributed to a defined population

label: Benchmarking
definition: Methodology comparing organizational practices against defined performance tiers or industry standards

label: Statistical Analysis
definition: Quantitative analysis of survey data including frequency distributions, cross-tabulations, and trend analysis

label: Field Research
definition: Supplementary telephone interviews with selected survey respondents for deeper qualitative insights

label: IT Budget Management
definition: Domain covering IT spending governance, cost optimization, budget processes, and alignment of IT investment with business strategy
